stat_of_week: 52 RBI - Athletic Park Dragons posted 52 RBI in a single week, tying the all-time season record set by BaseballPro77 in Week 4, driven by Jake McCarthy's 13 RBI, Dansby Swanson's 11 RBI, and James Wood's 7 RBI in a dominant 7–3 win over the Benchwarmers
thriller_teams: Canberra Wombats vs Wisconsin Rapids Reapers
thriller_note: The Wombats won despite being massively outgunned in HR (7 vs 14) and RBI (15 vs 28), surviving because Wisconsin Rapids logged zero quality starts and only 33.1 IP, ceding the pitching volume categories entirely — a warning sign for the Reapers heading into a Week 16 matchup against the Bombers.
key_storyline: Buttville Skidmarks delivered the most dominant single-week performance of the season, sweeping North Correa 10–0 behind 18 HR, 45 runs, and a suffocating 2.75 ERA with a 0.88 WHIP from the pitching staff — every single category went their way. Junior Caminero (4 HR, 7 RBI) and Rafael Devers (4 HR, 4 RBI) formed a historically devastating power duo, while Cole Young and Bryan Reynolds added depth up and down the lineup. With a 10–0 sweep — the most category wins in a single week this season — the Skidmarks have announced themselves as the most dangerous team in the league entering the playoff stretch.
lucky_reason: The Wombats won 6–4 despite being outscored in runs (25 vs 33), out-homered (7 vs 14), and out-RBI'd (15 vs 28) by Wisconsin Rapids, surviving solely because the Reapers' rotation collapsed to zero quality starts and just 33.1 innings pitched, gifting Canberra the pitching volume categories they needed.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs lost 6–3 to Sugar Land despite posting a superior ERA (4.27 vs 5.79), a better WHIP (1.19 vs 1.47), and comparable strikeout totals, but their 46.1 IP couldn't match the Skeeters' pitching volume and their offense ran dry at the worst time — fitting for the team with the worst luck delta in the league at –2.50.
records_broken: The Athletic Park Dragons tied the all-time season record for most RBI in a single week with 52, matching the mark set by BaseballPro77 in Week 4.
power_rankings: 1. Buttville Skidmarks | A 10–0 sweep is the most complete performance of the season — 18 HR, 45 R, Caminero and Devers both going 4 HR, and a 2.75 ERA/0.88 WHIP from the staff; this team has no weaknesses right now | up

2. Albuquerque Bombers | A 5–3 win over the Twins is steady if unspectacular — Freeman, Lindor, and Turang all contributed and Sandy Alcantara provided a quality start; the most consistent team in the league and a legitimate title favorite | same

3. BaseballPro77 | A 6–2 demolition of the Roughnecks backed by a league-best 2.36 ERA and 0.93 WHIP, with Cade Cavalli's near-perfect 0.14 WHIP and Jordan Walker's 7 runs leading the way; deep, balanced, and dangerous | same

4. Hiroshima Radiation | Won 6–4 over General Hospital on the strength of 83 K and 5 QS from the rotation — Nolan McLean and Taj Bradley were dominant — but only 4 HR from the offense remains the one hole in an otherwise elite roster | up

5. Athletic Park Dragons | Swanson (5 HR, 11 RBI), Wood (3 HR, 7 RBI), and McCarthy (3 HR, 13 RBI) combined for arguably the best offensive trio of the week, and the team tied the all-time RBI record; pitching was adequate enough for a 7–3 win, and this team is clicking at the right time | up

6. Riverside Roughnecks | Lost 6–2 to BaseballPro77 despite generating 72 hits and a solid pitching line from Casey Mize (14 K, 1.32 ERA) — the team simply couldn't win the power categories and got exposed; a luck delta of +2.88 means the record still needs scrutiny | down

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 5–3 to the Bombers despite Sean Burke (19 K, 2.38 ERA), Jesús Luzardo (9 K, 1.50 ERA), and Braxton Ashcraft (15 K, 1.20 WHIP) delivering elite pitching across the board; Yordan Alvarez (4 HR, 11 RBI) was heroic but the offense outside him remains thin | same

8. North Correa | Getting swept 10–0 by Buttville is brutal, but context matters — the Skidmarks were historically good this week; Michael Harris II (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Tristan Peters (6 R, .450 OBP) showed upside, and JP Sears adds rotation depth via the wire | down

9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kyle Stowers (3 HR, 7 RBI, 10 R) and Trea Turner (3 HR, 7 RBI) were outstanding, but zero quality starts and only 33.1 IP cost this team a winnable matchup against Canberra; the pitching-volume problem is not going away | same

10. Sugar Land Skeeters | Won 6–3 over the Wonderdogs behind Fernando Tatis Jr. and Kody Clemens leading the offense and five quality starts anchoring the pitching — but a 5.79 ERA, 1.47 WHIP, and .277 OBP are genuinely ugly numbers for a winning team; sustainable? No | same

11. General Hospital | Eury Pérez (16 K, 0.73 ERA, 0.49 WHIP) was one of the best individual pitching performances of the week and Kyle Tucker (.536 OBP) and Riley Greene (4 HR, 10 RBI) kept the offense alive, but losing 6–4 despite all that production confirms systemic pitching depth issues | down

12. Benchwarmers | Lost 7–3 to the Dragons despite a legitimately elite pitching line — Trey Yesavage (10 K, 2.13 ERA, 0.63 WHIP), Bryce Miller (0.29 WHIP), and Sonny Gray (1.50 ERA) were excellent — but the offense managed just 6 HR and the Dragons' power simply overwhelmed them | same

13. Canberra Wombats | Pete Crow-Armstrong (.615 OBP, 2 HR, 3 SB) and Jacob deGrom's return (9 K, 2.57 ERA, 0.57 WHIP) were genuine bright spots, but a 6–4 win that required the opponent to implode is not a blueprint; Aaron Nola's 7.94 ERA remains a serious concern | up

14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Lost 6–3 despite a better ERA, better WHIP, and Yoshinobu Yamamoto's strong return (10 K, 0.71 WHIP) — this team is the most snakebitten roster in the league with a –2.50 luck delta and faces Buttville in Week 16, which projects as an extremely difficult draw | down
